title: Issue with Docker-Compose with Podman 3.0.1
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

Docker-Compose fails to bring up Podman containers with Podman socket running.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. `sudo systemctl enable --now start podman.socket`

2. `sudo docker-compose -f path/to/docker-compose.yml up`

**Describe the results you received:**

```
OCI runtime create failed: this version of runc doesn't work on cgroups v2: unknown
```

**Describe the results you expected:**

The container to come up and be live.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

Happens everytime.

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:      3.0.1
API Version:  3.0.0
Go Version:   go1.15.8
Built:        Sat Feb 20 05:56:17 2021
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**


**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```
🐚 rpm -q podman
podman-3.0.1-1.fc33.x86_64
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide?**

Yes

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

Fedora Silverblue 33 on real hardware.
Docker-Compose version:
```
docker-compose version 1.28.5, build c4eb3a1f
docker-py version: 4.4.4
CPython version: 3.7.10
OpenSSL version: OpenSSL 1.1.0l  10 Sep 2019
```

comments.body: Does your compose file contains a build step?

> Does your compose file contains a build step?

There's no build stage.

Here's the compose file for context:

```yaml
version: "3.7"

services:
  flattrack_db:
    image: postgres:12.2-alpine
    restart: unless-stopped
    user: postgres
    ports:
      - '5432:5432'
    environment:
      - TZ=Pacific/Auckland
      - POSTGRES_DB=postgres
      - POSTGRES_USER=postgres
      - POSTGRES_PASSWORD=postgres
      - PGDATABASE=postgres
      - PGUSER=postgres
```

I assume rootfull containers run without issue?  And also,  it looks like runc is being used in a cgroups v2 environment.  sometimes that is a red herring for an error but perhaps there is something unique about your environment of config files?

> I assume rootfull containers run without issue? And also, it looks like runc is being used in a cgroups v2 environment. sometimes that is a red herring for an error but perhaps there is something unique about your environment of config files?

Rootful containers run fine in Podman - this error doesn't occur with `sudo podman run -it --rm -p 80:80 nginx:stable` for example.

Could it be `Docker-Compose` some how configuring the runtime?
I don't think so.  Is this a brand new install or did you have older versions of podman on this system.  Like stuff back to 1.x.

> I don't think so. Is this a brand new install or did you have older versions of podman on this system. Like stuff back to 1.x.

There were older versions. I've had this install since Fedora Silverblue 31.

@BobyMCbobs any chance that your configuration file might be hard coding runc? i.e. /etc/containers/libpod.conf or containers.conf?  on fedora, the original sits in /usr/share/containers ... if you have one in /etc/containers/, maybe rename it and see if you get further?
Both _libpod.conf_ and _containers.conf_ don't exist in _/etc/containers_. Copying _/usr/share/containers/containers.conf_ to _/etc/containers_ didn't seem to change anything
ok, one more thing to try,rename runc to something else for now and try again?
Can you also do a `podman inspect` on a container made by `docker-compose` and provide the output?
`podman info` is showing the correct default runtime, which makes me very curious.

> Can you also do a `podman inspect` on a container made by `docker-compose` and provide the output?

According to `sudo podman ps -a` after bringing the stack up, no containers appear.
Can you make containers with `podman run`? Do they work?

`sudo podman run -it --rm -p 80:80 --name nginx nginx:stable` works fine.
The _OCIRuntime_ is set to _crun_ according to `sudo podman inspect nginx | jq -r '.[0].OCIRuntime'`

@BobyMCbobs ok here's something to try. stop your sevice and socket . then in one terminal, do a  `sudo podman --log-level=debug system service -t0 unix://var/run/docker.sock`.  Then in another, run your compose...does this work ?

> @BobyMCbobs ok here's something to try. stop your sevice and socket . then in one terminal, do a `sudo podman --log-level=debug system service -t0 unix://var/run/docker.sock`. Then in another, run your compose...does this work ?

@baude, this appears to do it. This is good. The container comes up as expected.
Is there any chance you might have two different binaries of podman on your system?  or that the socket files are old?

> Is there any chance you might have two different binaries of podman on your system? or that the socket files are old?

Both my user and root are using the same version.

The socket file looks like this
```ini
[Unit]
Description=Podman API Socket
Documentation=man:podman-system-service(1)

[Socket]
ListenStream=%t/podman/podman.sock
SocketMode=0660

[Install]
WantedBy=sockets.target
```
and seems pretty normal to me.

I tried using docker-compose on Ubuntu 20.04 using kubic packages. Problem is the path `/var/run/docker.sock` does not exist. There is only `/var/run/podman/podman.sock`. Is this expected? What file do I need to change to make the socket on the correct path for docker-compose?

I found how to make the socket (maybe) work.

podman.socket
```
[Unit]
Description=Podman API Socket
Documentation=man:podman-system-service(1)

[Socket]
ListenStream=%t/docker.sock
SocketMode=0660

[Install]
WantedBy=sockets.target
```

podman.service
```
[Unit]
Description=Podman API Service
Requires=podman.socket
After=podman.socket
Documentation=man:podman-system-service(1)
StartLimitIntervalSec=0

[Service]
Type=exec
KillMode=process
Environment=LOGGING="--log-level=info"
ExecStart=/usr/bin/podman $LOGGING system service unix://var/run/docker.sock
```

And have docker-compose working. But do these changes stay after an APT upgrade?
Can you show what the difference is between your current unit files and the originals? We could look into doing this upstream if it's a solution

`ListenStream=%t/podman/podman.sock`
I changed to
`ListenStream=%t/docker.sock`


`ExecStart=/usr/bin/podman $LOGGING system service`
I changed to
`ExecStart=/usr/bin/podman $LOGGING system service unix://var/run/docker.sock`

I found that in Fedora 33 there is a package `podman-docker` available that installs a symlink /run/docker.sock -> /run/podman/podman.sock
This is the reason it works there but not in Ubuntu. In Kubic/Ubuntu there is no package with this name.
@lsm5 ^^

The missing socket is also a problem in opensuse. The unitfile is missing the docker.socket entry and creates a podman socket under /run. Is this something the packager should take care off? There is also no podman-docker package.

opensuse should ship a podman-docker package. Open up a bug with the distribution.
DOCKER_HOST environment variable is the other mechanism for this ot create a link between /run/docker.sock and /run/podman/podman.sock.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
I'm going to close this as it is the responsibility of the distributions to handle their packaging.  @lsm5 did you fix the kubic ubuntu packages?

Still no podman-docker package in kubic/Ubuntu.

In official Debian experimental repo there is now podman-docker package but this won't install a symlink.

Try download the `podman-docker` deb package and install it manually.

```bash
curl -v http://mirrors.cloud.tencent.com/ubuntu/pool/universe/libp/libpod/podman-docker_3.2.1+ds1-2ubuntu2_amd64.deb -O
sudo dpkg -i podman-docker_3.2.1+ds1-2ubuntu2_amd64.deb
sudo systemctl restart podman && sudo systemctl restart podman.socket
```

*Tested on Ubuntu Server 20.04.2 LTS*
